fundamentals.name: AST SpaceMobile, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: AST SpaceMobile, Inc., together with its subsidiaries, designs and develops the constellation of BlueBird satellites in the United States. The company provides a cellular broadband network in space to be accessible directly by smartphones for commercial use and other applications, as well as for government use. Its SpaceMobile service provides cellular broadband services to end-users who are out of terrestrial cellular coverage. The company was founded in 2017 and is headquartered in Midland, Texas.

judgment.summary: Extremely high revenue growth and strong cash position are offset by massive operating losses and a high valuation.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter missed EPS estimates by a wide margin (-0.66 vs -0.20); next earnings date is 2026-08-10.
positives: Revenue growth 1952.2% YoY — explosive top-line expansion.
Current ratio 18.47 and Quick ratio 17.91 — excellent short-term solvency.
Gross margin 44.8% — healthy unit economics on services provided.
negatives: Operating margin -1014.0% and Profit margin 0.0% — significant operational losses.
Forward P/E -376.30 and Price/Sales 510.93 — extremely high valuation multiples.
Free cash flow (TTM) $-1.41B — substantial cash burn.

news.paragraph: AST SpaceMobile experienced a significant 17% drop in its stock price following an explosion involving a Blue Origin rocket, which also impacted other space-related stocks. This event overshadowed the company's recent strong performance, as it had been the best-performing communication services stock over the past month. The explosion raises concerns about the broader space industry's operational risks and could affect investor sentiment towards ASTS.
